segments.s762-g9: A short nominal clause with the copula. ستاره‌شمر is a compound noun 'star‑reader/astrologer' serving as predicate; بود is the past copula 'was'. The conjunction و links that predicate to the following adjective phrase بسیارهوش, a fused adjective meaning 'very intelligent'. Word order here follows classical Persian: predicate + بود, then conjunction + adjectival comment.
segments.s762-g10: This is an inverted, somewhat idiomatic construction: به گفتارش marks the target of attention ('to/for his speech'), موبد is the agent ('the mobed/priest'), نهاده is the past participle/verb 'has placed' or 'lent', and دو گوش is the thing placed — literally 'two ears' but idiomatically 'full attention'. The object (دو گوش) follows the verb in classical word order; the whole clause means the mobed listened attentively to his words.
segments.s762-g11: برفت is an archaic past form meaning 'he went/departed' (classical narrative past). The conjunction و links the actions. گرانمایگان ('the noble/illustrious ones') is the definite object and is marked by the object marker را. ببرد here is a classical narrative past of بردن meaning 'he carried off/took away' or 'he led off.' The clause describes two successive actions: he went and he took away the nobles.
segments.s762-g12: هر آنکس که introduces a free relative 'whichever person who/ whoever'. که is the relative marker, and بودند is the past plural copula 'they were'. The adjectives بیدار و گرد follow and predicate the relative phrase: 'whoever were awake (بیدار) and گرد' (گرد here an adjective, archaic, meaning roughly 'active/alert/present'). This whole clause limits which nobles were taken.
segments.s762-g13: برین گونه is the fused classical locution 'بر این گونه' = 'in this manner / thus'. تا marks the temporal limit 'until', ماه here is 'month' and بگذشت is the past 'passed'. The numeral سی (thirty) follows the verb in older usage: 'until thirty months passed.' The order (verb before numeral) reflects classical poetic wording.
segments.s762-g14: همی is an archaic particle indicating continuous or repeated action ('kept on / continued'). رزم is the object 'battle/ fighting'. جستند is the past plural of جستن used here in the sense 'they engaged in / they fought' (classical poetic use). در قادسی is a locative phrase 'in Qadis(...)' (a place name, Qadisiyyah); in poetic diction the place name appears in the adjective form قادسی.
segments.s762-g15: This clause describes heavy casualties. بسی is a poetic quantifier 'many' (here used adverbially), کشته شد is a passive construction 'was/were killed' (کشته + شد), لشکر is the affected noun 'army/host' and از هر دو سوی is a prepositional phrase 'from/between both sides' specifying the sources of killing. The overall sense is 'many of the army were killed on both sides.'
segments.s762-g16: سپه یک ز دیگر نه begins a negative statement: سپه یک ز دیگر = 'one (army) from the other' (i.e. any army of the opposing side), نه (not) negates the verb. برگاشت is a narrative/archaic past used with روی (face) to express 'turned back / retreated' (literally 'lifted/changed face'); روی here is 'face' used idiomatically for 'turning/returning.' The clause asserts that none of the other armies turned away.
segments.s762-g17: بدانست is a past tense verb 'he knew/realized' placed before the subject (poetic inversion). رستم is the subject; شمار سپهر is the direct object (شمار 'number/count' governing سپهر 'heavens'), so the clause literally reads 'knew Rostam the number of the heavens.'
segments.s762-g18: ستاره‌شمر بود is a predicative phrase: بود is the past copula 'was' and ستاره‌شمر is the noun complement 'astrologer/ star‑counter.' The following و با داد و مهر is a coordinated phrase describing manner — 'and with justice and kindness' — where با introduces the manner/qualities.
segments.s762-g19: همی‌گفت is a habitual/imperfect past 'kept saying.' کاین introduces the subordinate clause 'that this…'. رزم را marks 'this battle' as the definite object; روی نیست literally 'there is no face' — a figurative expression meaning 'has no honour/standing' or 'is not proper.'
segments.s762-g20: ره آب شاهان is a genitive phrase 'the course/way of the kings' water' (ره 'course/way' governing آب شاهان). بدین جوی نیست is a predication: بدین ('in/along this') + جوی 'stream' + نیست 'is not', so the clause states that the kings' water‑course is not in/along this stream (i.e. this stream is unsuitable).
segments.s762-g21: بیاورد here is a past/habitual-perfect verb 'he brought' or 'he mustered/produced.' صلاب is the direct object 'firmness/strength.' The coordinate اختر گرفت follows: اختر 'a star' is object and گرفت 'took/seized' is the verb — a compact poetic sequence 'he brought strength and seized a star.'
segments.s762-g22: ز روز بلا is an ablative/time phrase 'from the day of calamity' (ز = 'from' in poetic style). دست بر سر گرفت is an idiomatic sequence: دست 'hand' + بر 'on/upon' + سر 'head' + گرفت 'he took/placed' — meaning 'he put his hand on his head' (a gesture of grief or lament).
segments.s762-g23: یکی نامه is an indefinite 'a certain/one letter' (یکی as 'a certain' or 'one'). سوی برادر marks direction 'to his brother.' به درد here means 'about (his) sorrow/affliction' where به introduces the topic and درد is emotional suffering.
segments.s762-g24: نوشت is the past verb 'he wrote.' The coordination و links the next clause سخن‌ها همه یاد کرد: سخن‌ها 'words/all matters' + همه 'all' and یاد کردن + کردن 'to recall/mention' (یاد کرد here = 'he mentioned/recounted'). So 'he wrote and recounted all the matters/words.'
segments.s762-g73: همی is an iterative/adverbial particle meaning 'always' or 'continually' placed before the verb; سرفرازند is third-person plural present ('they are triumphant/proud'). که here introduces the explanatory clause ('for/that'), not a relative; ایشان is the third-person plural pronoun 'they'. کیند is an archaic/persuasive present plural form (classical variant of کنـند/کنند) meaning 'they do' or 'they go/act' and links as the main verb of the subordinate clause introduced by که.
segments.s762-g74: به marks direction/target ('into/toward') and governs the place names ایران و مازنداران (Iran and Mazandaran); بر here has the sense 'against/onto' and combines with the verb چیند (present plural of چیدن) which in poetic/archaic use means 'seize, fall upon, pluck'—so بر چیند = 'fall upon/assail'. The clause therefore expresses the action for which they are proud: invading Iran and attacking Mazandaran.
segments.s762-g75: The repeated اگر ... اگر frames a conditional / concessive idea ('if ... if' ≈ 'whether ... or'), with مرز و راهست stating 'it is a border and a road' (راهست = copular adjectival phrase; -ست is an older copula). نیک و بد are paired adjectives 'good and bad' (i.e. 'whether favorable or unfavorable'). The whole segment sets up the general condition under which the following necessity holds.
segments.s762-g76: به ... به marks the means/instrument ('by/with'); گرز and شمشیر are the instruments ('mace/club' and 'sword'). باید is the impersonal/modal 'it must / one must', and ستد is an archaic present infinitive/3rd-person form meaning 'to take/seize' (here passive/impersonal: 'must be seized' or 'one must seize'). The clause therefore expresses necessity of taking/seizing by force.
segments.s762-g77: بکوشیم is first-person plural present/subjunctive/imperative 'let us strive' (the ب- prefix with -یم yields a hortative/request). و connects clauses. مردی is an indefinite/direct object 'a man' (or 'a brave man'), and به کار آوریم is a light-verb construction: به کار = 'into work/use' and آوریم (1pl present) 'we bring' → 'we deploy/put a man to work'.
segments.s762-g78: بریشان is the fused form of بر + ایشان ('upon/against them' or 'for them' depending on context) and marks the target affected. جهان تنگ و تار is the object phrase 'a world narrow and tar(–)' where تنگ = 'narrow' and تار here is an adjective meaning 'dark/bleak' in poetic usage (the literal listed senses do not include this poetic sense). آوریم (1pl) here has the sense 'cause/bring about' ('let us cause/make the world narrow and dark for them').
segments.s762-g79: نداند is third-person singular negative present 'does not know'; when combined with کسی (here interpreted as 'no one' because of the negation) the phrase means 'no one knows'. راز گردان‌سپهر is a poetic noun phrase 'the secret of the turning/revolving sky' (گردان‌سپهر is a compound describing the heavens in motion); the genitive/possessive relation is implied without an explicit ezāfe in this terser, poetic phrase.
segments.s762-g80: دگرگونه‌تر is the comparative form 'more different' (i.e. 'stranger' or 'changed'); گشت is the past/complete of گشتن 'became/turned'. بر ما = 'toward us' or 'upon us'. به مهر is an adverbial phrase 'by/with مهر' where به functions as 'by/with' and مهر here means 'favor/kindness' (so: 'it has become stranger toward us by favor', implying a change in the heavens' disposition toward us).
segments.s762-g49: برین is a fused demonstrative phrase (بر + این) meaning 'upon/over these'; سالیان is the plural 'years' and چارصد 'four hundred' follows the noun in this poetic word order. بگذرد is third-person singular present/subjunctive used futurally ('will pass'); the overall clause means 'about/four hundred years will pass over these years.'
segments.s762-g50: کزین (کز + این) = 'from this' introduces the following noun phrase; تخمه گیتی is a genitive construction 'seed/offspring of the world'; کسی with the negative verb نشمرد yields an impersonal negative 'no one will count' or 'no one will remain to be reckoned'. نشمرد is the negative present/imperative-form used here with future sense.
segments.s762-g51: ازیشان is a contracted prepositional phrase (از + ایشان) meaning 'from them'; فرستاده is the noun 'messenger' and serves as the subject before the verb آمد ('came'). به من is the dative 'to me'. The order Subject–Verb–Indirect object is normal in this poetic register.
segments.s762-g52: سخن رفت is an idiomatic, somewhat impersonal expression meaning 'a talk/speech took place' or 'it was spoken'; رفت is the verb 'go' used here to mean 'was said/occurred'. هرگونه modifies the manner 'in every way/kind' and بر انجمن ('on/at the assembly') gives the locus—classical use of بر for 'before/at'.
segments.s762-g53: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/so that'; از indicates the starting point 'from'; قادسی is a place-name or adjective meaning 'Qadsi/pertaining to Quds' and تا ... لب رودبار is a range 'as far as the lip/brink of Rudbar' (تا = 'as far as'). The whole phrase sets the territorial limits.
segments.s762-g54: زمین را is the definite object marked by را ('the land'); ببخشیم is first-person plural present/subjunctive ('let us give' / 'we should grant') used optatively or to express an intended action. با شهریار literally reads 'with the king'—here indicating the recipient/association (i.e., granting the land to/into the keeping of the shah) rather than strictly 'together with.'
segments.s762-g55: وزان سو is a compound 'and from that side' (وزان = و + زان 'and from that'); یکی is the numeral 'one'; برگشاییم is first-person plural subjunctive/optative 'let us open' or 'we will open'; راه is the object 'road'—poetic order places the object after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s762-g56: به شهری = 'to a city' (به as directional 'to/into'); کجا here functions as a relative 'where' and هست is the copula 'there is', so کجا هست بازارگاه forms a relative clause 'where there is a marketplace' modifying شهری. The word order places the relative clause after the noun it qualifies.
segments.s762-g25: نخست is an adverb 'first/firstly' placed before the verb phrase. آفرین کرد is a light-predicate construction: آفرین (praise/acclamation) + کرد (past of کردن ‘to do/make’) = 'gave praise' or 'praised'. بر + کردگار marks the beneficiary/target with the preposition بر meaning 'to/toward' and کردگار is the Creator/God.
segments.s762-g26: کزو is the compound prepositional/pronominal form 'from whom / from which' linking this clause to the preceding praise (i.e. 'from whom he perceived'). دید is the simple past 'saw/perceived'. نیک و بد form a paratactic pair 'good and bad' modifying روزگار, here 'the fortunes/time/fate of the age' perceived by him.
segments.s762-g27: دگر گفت here means 'again/then he said' (دگر as an adverb). گفت is the verb 'said' (the provided sense list did not include the verbal sense, so it is marked separately below). کز introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that/from which' and گردش آسمان is a genitive phrase 'the turning/revolution of the heavens' giving the cause or source.
segments.s762-g28: پژوهنده‌مردم is a compound: پژوهندهٔ مردم 'one who examines/observes people'. شود is the present-future/auxiliary 'becomes' (شدن in simple form). بدگمان is a predicative adjective 'suspicious/distrustful', so the clause means 'becomes suspicious'.
segments.s762-g29: گنه‌کارتر is the comparative form ('more sinful' / 'more a sinner'), formed by the adjective گنه‌کار + comparative -تر; it functions as predicate. در زمانه 'in the age/era' sets the circumstantial context, and منم is the emphatic first-person copula 'I am' (من + enclitic -م).
segments.s762-g30: ازیرا is an archaic/contracted connective (here to be read as 'از این رو' = 'for this reason' / 'therefore'); it introduces the consequence. گرفتار is a predicate/adjective 'caught/seized/afflicted'. آهرمنم is 'I am of Ahriman' or 'I am seized by Ahriman' with the divine/diabolic name (آهرمن = Ahriman) plus the 1st-person suffix -م.
segments.s762-g31: که here introduces an explanatory clause 'for/that' giving the reason. این خانه 'this house' is the subject; از + پادشاهی expresses separation/privation 'of/from kingship' and تهیست is a single-word predicate 'is empty / is devoid (of)'. The construction states a causal fact ('that this house lacks kingship').
segments.s762-g32: نه ... و ... ساختار منفیِ مقایسه‌ای دارد: نه هنگام پیروزی و فرهیست = 'it is neither the time of victory nor of distinction'. هنگام پیروزی = 'the time/season of victory'. فرهیست is a predicate meaning 'is illustrious/noble' or here 'is a time of glory/fame', completing the negation.
segments.s762-g33: ز + noun (ز چارم) gives a source/locative sense 'from my fourth' (چارم = 'my fourth' here, an astrological/positional sense). همی‌بنگرد is a classical present/habitual form with the prefix همی- (continuous/ongoing) + بنگرد (بنـ- a variant of 'بنگرد' = 'looks/peers'); word order is poetic: preposition phrase + verb + subject (آفتاب) so literally 'from my fourth looks the sun.'
segments.s762-g34: کزین is an archaic contraction of 'که ز این' meaning 'for/from this' (introducing a cause). جنگ is the noun 'war/battle' and ما را is 'to/for us' with را as an object/affected-party marker; بد here modifies آید (it 'comes badly' or 'unfavorably'), and شتاب is the noun 'haste' which is the thing that 'comes' (آید). The clause is impersonal-causal: 'because of this battle a bad haste comes upon us.'
segments.s762-g35: ز marks source ('from Bahram and from Venus'). The form زهره‌ست here contains the copula -ست ('is') attached to زهره, producing 'and from Venus is' — the clause as a whole states the source of harm. ما را uses را to mark the affected party ('to us'), and گزند is the noun 'harm.' The line thus means 'harm to us comes from Bahram and Venus.'
segments.s762-g36: نشاید is an impersonal/modal negative meaning 'it is not fitting/allowed'; it governs the following infinitive گذشتن ('to pass'). ز چرخ بلند is a prepositional phrase 'beyond/from the high heaven' (چرخ = sky/heaven/fate, بلند = high). The structure is 'نشاید + infinitive + complement' = 'it is not fitting to pass beyond the high heaven.'
segments.s762-g37: همان is a demonstrative/adverb meaning 'that/the same.' تیر and کیوان are heavenly names placed in coordination 'Tir and Kayvān'; برابر شدست is an archaic perfective/passive-like construction (شد + ست) meaning 'have become equal' or 'are opposite/aligned.' Word order is subject(s) + predicate (برابر شدست).
segments.s762-g38: عطارد is the subject 'Mercury.' به برج is the locative/directional phrase 'into/at the (zodiac) sign' (به = into/towards; برج = zodiac sign here). پیکر 'body/figure' combined with شدست ('has become') yields 'has become (of) two figures' (the text's 'دو' is understood in the line); شدست is the same archaic 'has become' form. Overall: 'Mercury in the sign has become two‑formed.'
segments.s762-g39: چنین است is a declarative 'thus it is.' و links the clauses. کاری بزرگست uses کاری ('a matter/affair' — note the lemma senses given in the data are mismatched to this usage) + بزرگست ('is great') to mean 'there is a great thing/affair.' پیش here means 'ahead/before' (i.e. 'in front of us' or 'forthcoming').
segments.s762-g40: همی is the prefix/adverb indicating continual or emphatic aspect ('continually/indeed'). سیر گردد is a verbal phrase where سیر here has a sense of movement/roaming (the poetic 'to go about') and گردد (an older subjunctive/Present form of شدن/گشتن) = 'becomes/goes' — together 'the heart goes away/roams.' دل is the subject 'heart.' از جان خویش is a prepositional phrase 'from its own life' (خویش = 'his/its own' with ش as possessive clitic). So: 'the heart continually departs/roams away from its life.'
segments.s762-g129: This is a twofold imperative addressed to 'تو' (poetic 'thou'): 'تو پدرود باش' = 'be paderud' and coordinated 'بی‌آزار باش' = 'be harmless'. 'پدرود' here is used predicatively (a noun meaning 'farewell/peace') after the subject and before the copular imperative 'باش'. The repeated 'باش' gives two coordinated commands; the explicit subject 'تو' is emphatic/poetic.
segments.s762-g130: 'ز بهر' is a poetic prepositional phrase 'for the sake of / on behalf of'. It governs 'تن شه' ('the king's body/person'); in classical Persian the ezāfe is often omitted in verse, so 'تن شه' reads as 'the king's body'. 'به تیمار باش' is another imperative clause where 'به' marks the relation ('in/for care') and 'تیمار' is the nominal object of 'باش' meaning 'care/solicitude.'
segments.s762-g131: The clause begins with the conditional particle 'گر' = 'if'. 'او را بد آید' uses the object-marker 'را' with 'او' and the verb 'آید' (here 'befalls/happens') to mean 'if bad/unfavourable (bad fortune) comes to him'. The following imperative 'تو شو پیش اوی' literally 'be thou before him' uses 'پیش' + the clitic/enclitic 'اوی' to indicate 'in his presence/before him'; 'شو' is the singular imperative 'become/go'.
segments.s762-g132: Imperative 'بسپار' (leave/entrust/consign) takes an instrument/goal marked by 'به' here meaning 'by/with' (i.e. 'to the sword'): 'به شمشیر بسپار' = 'deliver to the sword'. 'پرخاش‌جوی' is the accusative noun (no 'را' required in poetry) identifying whom to consign — 'the aggressor'.
segments.s762-g133: 'چو' here functions as a temporal/conditional 'when'. Word order is poetic: 'با تخت منبر برابر کنند' = 'they make (it) level/opposite the pulpit with the throne' (literally 'with throne pulpit equal make'); the verb 'کنند' is 3rd-person plural subjunctive/compound-present placed at the end as is normal in Persian. 'با' marks the accompanying noun and 'برابر' means 'level/opposite' in this collocation.
segments.s762-g134: Nominal object + verb idiom 'نام ... کردن' = 'to name/call (someone) X'. 'همه' is the subject 'all/everyone' and 'کنند' is the verb placed at the end. The two proper names 'بوبکر و عمر' are the names used; the object being named is omitted in the line (understood from context).
segments.s762-g135: 'تبه گردد' is a poetic formula meaning 'it will become ruinous/woeful' or 'woe will come' — 'گردد' is the present/future-forming verb 'become'. The phrase that follows, 'این رنج‌های دراز', is the patient/topic 'these long sufferings' (plural marker '-ها' attached), placed after the verb by poetic inversion.
segments.s762-g136: This is a nominal clause: 'نشیبی درازست' = 'a long slope/descent there is' where 'درازست' is the fused adjective + copula ('دراز است') used in verse. 'پیش فراز' is a prepositional phrase 'before the summit/top' that modifies where the long descent lies. Word order is compact/poetic but follows standard relations: noun + adjective+copula, then prepositional modifier.
segments.s762-g57: بدان تا is a purpose clause head: ب (the prefix) + دان (imperative/infinitive 'know' / 'let it be so') together introduce the clause 'so that'. تا then signals purpose 'so that / in order that'. خریم و فروشیم are first-person plural verb forms ('we buy' / 'we sell'), placed after the purpose marker, and چیز is the object 'things/goods'. Word order is typical Persian: purpose marker + verb phrases + object.
segments.s762-g58: ازین پس is a temporal phrase 'from this on / henceforth' (از + ین as orthographic 'این' + پس). فزونی is the noun 'increase/excess' and نجوییم is a present-tense first‑person‑plural verb with the negative prefix ن- ('we do not seek/ask'). نیز 'also' / 'either' reinforces the statement. The clause thus states a continuing refusal to seek surplus.
segments.s762-g59: پذیریم is first‑person plural (subjunctive/indicative) 'we accept / we agree'. ما is the explicit subject 'we' (emphatic). ساو here most likely names a place (Saveh) or party being referred to; و links that to باژ (an older spelling/word related to levies/tribute or 'cry') and گران qualifies the latter as 'heavy / costly'. The line lists what they accept or the parties/items involved before the following refusal.
segments.s762-g60: نجوییم again is 'we do not seek / we will not demand' (ن- negative + جوییم). دیهیم is a noun meaning 'villagers / rural people' and کنداوران are 'diggers / excavators' (people who dig or search the ground). Grammatically this clause attaches to the preceding acceptance: although we accept X, we will not press or demand it from the ordinary villagers/diggers.
segments.s762-g61: شهنشاه را is the definite object ('the Shahanshah' marked by را). نیز 'also' adds that this action applies as well. فرمان is 'command/order' and بریم is first‑person plural (subjunctive/indicative) of بردن 'to take/carry/obey' — here 'we will carry out / obey'. The verb follows the object and completes the clause in standard Persian SOV order with the object fronted by را.
segments.s762-g62: گر is the conditional 'if'. از ما 'of/from us' marks the agent to whom the request would be addressed. بخواهد is third‑person singular present/subjunctive 'he wishes/asks (that)'. گروگان is the object 'a hostage' and بریم (we will take) completes the conditional 'if he asks of us, we will take a hostage.'
segments.s762-g63: چنین است is a formula 'thus it is / such is (it)'. گفتار و کردار are the coordinated nouns 'speech and conduct/deed'. نیست after them functions to negate existence ('there is not / there is nothing (more)'); the line sets up that, apart from what follows, words and deeds amount to this alone.
segments.s762-g64: جز ... نیست is the idiom 'there is nothing except / nothing but ...'. از is the preposition 'of/from', and گردش کژ پرگار is a noun phrase 'the crooked/perverse turning/rotation of the compass' (پرگار literally 'drawing compass', here a metaphor). The whole clause therefore means 'there is nothing except the perverse rotation of the compass' — i.e., nothing but a distorted, errant course.
segments.s762-g65: برین is composed of بر + ین (an orthographic/contracted form of این) and here means 'on/at this (place)'. نیز follows to mean 'also'. جنگی بود هر زمان is a nominal clause with بود in the past ('there was') and هر زمان ('at every time' / 'always') modifying it: 'there was also fighting at all times.'
segments.s762-g66: که introduces a subordinate/relative clause ('that / when'). کشته شود is a passive or stative verbal phrase in the present/subjunctive-formative (شود used as 'be' with the past participle کشته): 'is killed / should be killed / when one is killed'. صد هژبر دمان is the clause's object/phrase naming the quantity and person/place involved; هژبر is treated here as a proper name/epithet and دمان as the following name/place element.
segments.s762-g67: بزرگان is the plural noun 'great ones / chiefs'. که + clause ('who') forms a relative clause: با من به جنگ اندرند = 'who are with me in war' or 'who are engaged in battle with me'. اندرند is an archaic present plural copula/locative-like verb meaning 'they are / are engaged'.
segments.s762-g68: به گفتار ایشان is an adverbial prepositional phrase meaning 'by / according to their speech'. Here به is used with the instrumental/criterial sense 'by'. همی‌ننگرند is a present habitual/plural verb 'they look / they regard / they judge' (همی- marks ongoing or habitual action).
segments.s762-g69: چو می‌روی here means 'when / as you go' (چو = 'when/as'), and the clause gives two alternatives: طبری و چون ارمنی 'as a Tabari and as an Armenian' (چون repeated to mark the second alternative). می‌روی is 2nd-person singular present 'you go'.
segments.s762-g70: به جنگ‌اند is an idiom meaning 'they are at war' (به + جنگ + اند, with -اند marking 3rd‑pl.). با introduces the adversary/partner 'with', and کیش آهرمنی means 'the Ahrimanic / demonic sect/creed' (آهرمنی = pertaining to Ahriman/the demonic).
segments.s762-g71: چو introduces a simile 'like / as'. گلبوی سوری is a compound describing a rosy/Syrian thing/person (گلبوی 'rose-scented / rose-like', سوری 'Syrian' here), and این مهتران 'these chiefs' picks up another comparison / list item: 'and these chiefs'. مهتران is plural of مهتر 'chief/leader'.
segments.s762-g72: که introduces a relative clause describing the chiefs: کوپال دارند 'they have Kūpāl' — here کوپال is read as the name of a (named) weapon or distinguishing item — and گرز گران 'and heavy maces' (گرز = mace/club, گران = heavy / weighty). دارند is the present plural 'they have/possess'.
segments.s762-g209: 'چو' is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'when' introducing a temporal clause. The prepositional phrase 'بر تخمه‌ای' ('over/against a seed/offspring') comes before the verb; the verb 'بگذرد' (3sg) means 'passes' and is placed before its subject 'روزگار' (a common poetic inversion: verb before subject). 'بگذرد' is the present/indicative (general-time) form used to express 'when X passes'.
segments.s762-g210: 'چه سود آید' is a rhetorical question: 'what benefit comes'. 'چه' = 'what' modifies 'سود' (benefit), and 'آید' is the 3sg verb 'comes/happens'. The phrase 'از رنج وز کارزار' means 'from suffering and from battle' — in classical Persian 'وز' functions as an archaic 'و + زِ' (and from), carrying the preposition 'از' over the second noun; here 'وز' is an archaism rather than the literal senses listed.
segments.s762-g211: 'تو را ای برادر' is a vocative address: 'you, O brother'; the particle 'را' here is used as a topical/emphatic marker before the vocative (not the ordinary direct-object marker). 'تن آباد باد' is an optative/wish: 'تن آباد' = 'body (be) well/prosperous' and final 'باد' is the optative particle ('may it be'). Word order places the wish-predicate after the vocative.
segments.s762-g212: 'دل شاه ایران' is a genitive noun chain 'the heart of the Shah of Iran'. 'به تو' is a benefactive/prepositional phrase 'for you' and 'شاد باد' is another optative clause meaning 'may [it] be glad' with 'باد' marking the wish. The copula is omitted (typical in poetry) and the optative applies to 'دل'.
segments.s762-g213: 'که' here introduces an explanatory clause ('for' / 'that'), giving the reason or explanation that follows. 'این قادسی' = 'this sacred/holy [place]' (an epithet). 'گورگاه منست' = 'is my tomb' — 'منست' is an archaic/poetic fused form meaning 'is mine' (literally 'من است') used as the copula/predication 'belongs to me / is mine'.
segments.s762-g214: This line strings nominal phrases with an omitted copula until the final 'منست' supplies the predication. 'کفن جوشن' literally pairs 'shroud' and 'jows̲han' (cuirass/armor) meaning 'my shroud is mail/armor', and 'خون کلاه' pairs 'blood' with 'helmet' meaning 'blood is my helmet'. The final 'منست' ('is mine') applies to both nominal pairs; the syntax reflects poetic ellipsis and parallelism.
segments.s762-g215: 'چنین است' = 'thus is' places the commentive adverb before the copula for emphasis. 'راز سپهر بلند' = 'the secret of the lofty sky/heavens' (genitive relationship: رازِ سپهرِ بلند). The line asserts that what has been said is the nature ('secret') of the high heavens; word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s762-g216: This is an imperative negative: 'تو ... مبند' = 'do not close (bind)'. 'تو' is the (implicit) addressee, 'دل را' is the definite/marked object 'the heart', 'به درد من' = 'to/onto my pain' (benefactive/targeting prepositional phrase), and 'اندر' is the classical preposition 'in/into'. 'مبند' is negative imperative (م + بند) = 'do not shut/close', so whole: 'Do not shut your heart against my sorrow.'
segments.s762-g177: The repeated negator نه ... نه ... lists items that are excluded; there is no finite verb here (a nominal clause) so the negation applies to an implied state (‘there is no …’). The nouns follow a simple coordination pattern: جشن (festival) and رامش (tranquility) joined by و, then another coordinated pair کوشش (effort) and کام (desire).
segments.s762-g178: همه چاره is a nominal phrase meaning ‘the whole/only remedy’ (همه modifying چاره). ورزش و ساز دام are two coordinated nouns giving that remedy: ورزش (‘exercise/practice’) and ساز دام (‘the device/means of a trap’), where ساز is used in the sense ‘device/means’ and دام ‘trap’. The verb is omitted; this clause completes the thought begun by the negations above.
segments.s762-g179: پدر با پسر is a noun + prepositional phrase (‘the father with the son’). کین سیم (here presented as part of the object) names what was brought; the finite verb آورد is simple past 3rd-person singular ‘brought’. In classical/poetic order objects may be enumerated before the verb as here.
segments.s762-g180: This is another object list followed by the same past verb آورد: خورش (stew), کشک (dried-yogurt), و (and), پوشش گلیم — here پوشش (‘covering’) and گلیم (‘kilim’) function as apposition: ‘a covering, a kilim’. The verb آورد again is past 3rd-singular 'brought.'
segments.s762-g181: زیان کسان is a possessive construction meaning ‘the harm/loss of people’ (possessed before possessor). از پی + noun means ‘in pursuit of / after’ so از پی سود خویش = ‘in pursuit of their own profit.’ خویش is expressed here as خوی + ش (root + possessive suffix); ش is the possessive suffix ‘his/their/one’s own.’
segments.s762-g182: بجویند is 3rd-person-plural present (they seek/search). The clause continues with و (and) then دین (religion) as object of اندر آرند پیش: اندر is a locative/prepositional marker (‘in/among/before’ here linking to پیش), آرند (3rd-plural present of آورد) ‘they bring/put’, and پیش ‘before/in front of’. Grammatically this says they seek harm and set religion forward (i.e., put religion before).
segments.s762-g183: نباشد is a negative-form verb meaning ‘is not’ / ‘does not exist/appear’ applied to the following nouns; بهار و زمستان are coordinated subjects (‘spring and winter’). پدید is a verb/adjective ‘manifest/appear’; word order here places the negated copular verb before the subjects and the predicate.
segments.s762-g184: نیارند here functions as a negative 3rd-plural verb ‘they do not bring’ (negative of آورد/آرند) and takes هنگام رامش ‘at the time of repose’ as a temporal phrase; نبید (at the end) is the object ‘wine’. So the clause says that at the hour of rest they do not bring wine. (Note: the listed lexical senses for نیارند in the supplied data do not match this verbal use, so a sense index is not applicable.)
segments.s762-g169: The repeated negator نه before each noun creates a correlative negation ('neither ... nor ... nor'). The copula بود (past 'was') follows the list, giving a past-state judgment about the existence of such people. Each noun (دهقان، ترک، تازی) is an ethnic/occupational noun listed in the subject position.
segments.s762-g170: سخن‌ها (the plural subject, marked by -ها) starts the clause; the locative/comparative phrase به کردار بازی ('in the manner/conduct of play') follows and functions as the predicate-complement. بود is the past copula again. Note the adjective-like construction کردار بازی = 'conduct/behavior of play' where کردار modifies the sense of بازی.
segments.s762-g171: همه گنج‌ها is the indefinite/collective subject 'all the treasures'. زیر دامن is a prepositional phrase 'under the hem/skirt' (دامن here literal or figurative 'protection'), and نهند is the classic narrative/3rd‑person plural form of نهاد 'to place/put' (they put/placed). Word order is subject + adverbial phrase + verb.
segments.s762-g172: بمیرند is the 3rd‑person plural present/subjunctive/jussive form of مردن used here narratively to mean 'they die/perish'. The conjunction و links clauses; کوشش به دشمن دهند literally 'they give/hand over effort to the enemy' with دهند (3pl) as the verb and به marking the beneficiary 'to the enemy'. The implicit subject remains the same 'they'.
segments.s762-g173: بود opens the clause ('there was'). دانشومند و زاهد are coordinated nouns 'a learned man and an ascetic.' The short phrase به نام here means 'for the sake of a name/reputation' (به + نام), indicating motive rather than literal naming. The sequence frames the type of person and his aim.
segments.s762-g174: بکوشد is the 3rd‑person singular imperfect/subjunctive of کوشیدن 'to strive/attempt' ('he strove'). ازین ('از' + 'ین' = 'from this') gives the starting point 'from then/from this,' تا که introduces the purpose/result clause 'until that' and آید به کام means 'he comes to his desire' (آید = 'attain/come', به کام = 'to the wish/desire'). Word order places the temporal/prepositional element between verb and subordinate clause as is common in classical Persian.
segments.s762-g175: چنان ... گردد ... introduces a result clause meaning 'so ... that ...'. فاش گردد is an impersonal/inchoative construction 'become revealed/exposed' (گردد here is subjunctive/predicative), and the coordinated nouns غم و رنج و شور are the things made manifest: grief, suffering and tumult/passion.
segments.s762-g176: که introduces the result clause 'that ...'. شادی is the resultant noun 'joy'; به هنگام is the temporal preposition 'at the time (of)', followed by the proper name بهرام and the epithet گور (here 'Gūr' = 'the Onager', part of the name Bahram Gūr). The phrase thus specifies the time/reference for the joy mentioned.
segments.s762-g153: جمع‌بندی فعل و وصف: پیاده (predicate/adverb/adjective) + شود (فعل بیانی/وقوع) و سپس مفعول/موضوع مردم جنگ‌جوی می‌آید. ساختار «X شود Y» در نظم شعر کلاسیک به‌معنای «Y تبدیل به X می‌شود» یا «Y به‌صورت X درآید». جنگ‌جوی صفتِ مردم است و شود زمان/حالت را بیان می‌کند.
segments.s762-g154: سوار (نَعت یا اسم) به‌عنوان موضوع یا معرفِ شخصِ سوار است؛ آنک شکل نسبی/موصولی («که/آن‌که») است و آن را به فعل بعدی وصل می‌کند. «لاف آرد» ترکیبِ فعل + مفعول است به معنی «می‌بالد/افشا می‌کند/پر‌مدعاست» و «و گفت‌وگوی» پیوستِ اسمی است (گفت‌وگو = سخن‌گفتن/حرف‌زدن). فعل صریح در این بخش نیست؛ نسبت به مصرع قبلی باید تکمیل معنایی شود (شکلِ توصیفی/کنایه‌ای).
segments.s762-g155: ترکیب اسمی-فعلی: «کشاورز (اسم) جنگی (صفتِ فعل‌ساز) شود» یعنی «کشاورز به جنگی‌بودن درمی‌آید/می‌شود جنگجو». بی‌هنر پس از شود صفتِ تبعی است که حالتِ ناتوانی یا فقدان مهارتِ تازه‌وارد را توصیف می‌کند. شود فعل گزارش‌گرِ تبدیل است (مصدر/ماضی نقلی صوری در نظم حماسی).
segments.s762-g156: فهرستِ متوالیِ مؤلفه‌ها: «نژاد و هنر کمتر آید» یعنی «نسب/نژاد و مهارت(ها) کاهش یابند/کم شوند». فعل «آید» به‌معنای «حاصل شدن/پدید آمدن/شدن» به کار رفته و «کمتر» صفتِ تقلیل است. کلمهٔ پایانی «ببر» اینجا به‌صورت خطاب (وجه ندا) یا تشبیه تصویری است — به‌عنوانِ نامِ حیوان (ببر) خوانده می‌شود، نه امری — و جمله را با آدرسِ شاعرانه ختم می‌کند.
segments.s762-g157: فعلِ پیوستهٔ شعری: «رباید همی» فعل مرکب است که «ربودن/رباییدن» را به‌صورت خبری/عمومی بیان می‌کند و «همی» تأکیدی است (دلالت بر استمرار یا عادتی بودن). ترتیب «این از آن آن ازین» سبکِ شعریِ تکراری و قرینه‌سازی است: «این (چیز) را از آن (چیز) رباید، آن (چیز) را از این (چیز)» — یعنی از همدیگر گرفته خواهد شد.
segments.s762-g158: حرفِ ربط/سبب «ز» (=به‌خاطر / از) علت را می‌آورد؛ «نفرین» مفعول/موضوع علت است. «ندانند» منفیِ جمعِ خبر است به‌معنای «نمی‌دانند/معرفی نمی‌کنند»، و «باز آفرین» عبارتِ اسمی است: «آفرین» (=ستایش/تحسین) با «باز» (=دگر/دوباره)؛ کلیت می‌شود «به‌خاطر نفرین، دیگر ستایش را نمی‌شناسند» یا «نتوانند ستایش را بازخوانند». ساختار فشردهٔ شعری مرتب است و فعلِ جمله «ندانند» است.
segments.s762-g159: مقایسهٔ وصفی: «نهان بدتر از آشکارا شود» ساختارِ مقایسه‌ایِ فشرده دارد: «نهان (چیزهای نهفته) بدتر از آشکارا (از چیزهای آشکار) می‌شود». «بدتر» صفتِ مقایسه‌ای است و «شود» فعلِ گزارش‌کنندهٔ تبدیل است. ترتیب کلمات در شعر کلاسیک می‌تواند بر وزن و ایجاز تأکید کند؛ معنا همان «پنهان بدتر از آشکار است/درآید» است.
segments.s762-g160: ساختار مرکبِ اسمی-فعلی: «دلِ شاه‌شان (قلبِ پادشاهِ ایشان) سنگ خارا شود» یعنی «قلبِ پادشاه‌شان همچون سنگِ سخت/خارا گردد». «سنگ خارا» ترکیبِ تشبیهی است (سنگ + خارا = سنگِ سخت/چخماق‌گون) و «شود» باز هم فعلِ تبدیل را می‌آورد؛ ترتیب صفت و اسم شعری است.
segments.s762-g97: درودش ده is an imperative clause: ده is the imperative 'give' and درودش is درود 'greeting' plus the 3rd-person object clitic -ش 'to him'. از ما is a prepositional phrase 'from us'. و links the two objects; بسیار modifies پند ('many' or 'many a'), so the full segment means 'give him our greeting and many advices.'
segments.s762-g98: بدان is the verb be‑ + دان here meaning 'consider it' or 'take this to be so' (imperative/jussive sense). تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that'. نباشد is the negative subjunctive/optative (third person) meaning 'may there not be' / 'so that there is not'. به گیتی is a locative 'in the world'. نژند is a predicative adjective 'base/despised'.
segments.s762-g99: گر is the conditional 'if'. از من is the postpositional phrase 'about/from me'. بدآگاهی here means 'bad information/ill-report' and آرد is the 3rd-person singular present of آوردن 'brings'. کسی is the indefinite 'someone/anyone' — the whole = 'if anyone brings ill-report about me'.
segments.s762-g100: مباش is the negative imperative 'do not be' (2nd-person prohibitive). اندرین کار = 'in this affair' (اندرین = 'in this/among these' + کار 'matter/affair'). غمگین بسی: غمگین 'sorrowful' modified by بسی (an intensifier/adverb meaning 'much' / 'very' or 'sufficingly'), so the clause prohibits being greatly saddened about the matter.
segments.s762-g101: چنان دان is an instruction 'consider it thus' (چنان 'so/thus' + دان 'consider/know'). که introduces a following illustrative clause. اندر is the preposition 'in/within'. سرای here means 'house/room/storehouse' (archaic usage) and سپنج is 'sponge' or 'sponge-like thing' — together 'in a sponge-house' (a metaphorical storage place).
segments.s762-g102: کسی is 'someone'. کو in classical/poetic Persian functions as a relative/connecting particle 'who/that' (archaic here), linking to the preceding 'someone'. نهد is the 3rd-person singular present of نهادن 'places/sets'. گنج is the direct object 'treasure'. با دست رنج is an instrumental/genitive phrase 'with the hand of toil' (با 'with' + دست 'hand' + رنج 'toil/hardship'), meaning the treasure was placed by a hand made weary by effort.
segments.s762-g103: چو here means 'when'. گاه 'time/occasion'. آیدش is 'comes to him' (آید + enclitic -ش indicating 'to him' or 'for him'), i.e. 'when his time comes'. زین جهان 'from this world' (زین is a poetic form); بگذرد is the 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive of گذشتن 'passes' — the sequence expresses 'when his time comes, he passes away from this world'.
segments.s762-g104: از آن رنج is a preposed phrase 'from/of that hardship'. او is the pronoun 'he' (referring to the person who dies). دیگری is 'another/other person'. برخورد here is used intransitively in the sense 'meets/comes upon/encounters' (someone else will meet or suffer that hardship). Word order emphasizes the hardship (از آن رنج) before stating who will encounter it.
segments.s762-g105: همیشه (always) is an adverb placed at the start for general injunction. به یزدان‌پرستان is a prepositional phrase 'toward the God‑worshippers' (به = toward + یزدان‌پرستان = the pious); گرای here is an imperative/verbal notion 'incline/turn' (classical poetical usage) commanding the addressee to direct themselves toward the pious.
segments.s762-g106: بپرداز is an imperative meaning 'attend to/turn' (pay attention or turn away). دل is the direct object 'the heart' (what to turn). زین here introduces the following noun phrase ('from/away from this…' in classical usage) and سپنجی‌سرای names the place 'sponge‑house' (bath/inn); together the phrase commands: 'turn your heart away from this sponge‑house.' Word order (verb + object + modifier) is typical of poetic Persian imperatives.
segments.s762-g107: که introduces a subordinate temporal/clausal clause ('when' / 'that'). آمد is the past tense 'came/occurred' used impersonally with the following locative phrase. به تنگ اندرون روزگار is an idiomatic locative: به تنگ = 'pressed/narrowed', اندرون = 'inner quarters/within', روزگار = 'time/fate' — together: 'when fate/fortune pressed my inner life.'
segments.s762-g108: نبیند is the negative present/past form of 'to see' used here narratively ('did not see' / 'saw not'). مرا (written here split as م + را) is the object 'me' (را is the definite/object marker attached). زین سپس is a temporal adverbial 'after this/thereafter' and شهریار is the subject 'the king' placed at the end; the whole line reads in English order: 'thereafter the king did not see me.'
segments.s762-g109: تو is the addressee 'you'. با هر که is a pattern 'with whoever/whomever' (با = with, هر که = whoever). از دوده ما بود literally 'was from our soot/lineage' — دوده ما is a figurative or clan reference 'of our household/origin'. بود is the past copula 'was' linking the relative phrase to the head.
segments.s762-g110: اگر ... اگر here functions as a correlative pair 'whether ... or ...' rather than a conditional. پیر = 'old', مرد = 'man/person' and برنا = 'youthful/vigorous'; بود (again) is the copula applying to both members: 'whether he was old or a vigorous young man.'
segments.s762-g111: همه 'everyone' is the vocative/imperative addressee. پیش یزدان is 'in the presence of God' (پیش = before/in presence of + یزدان = God). نیایش کنید is a plural imperative 'perform worship/offer prayer' (کنید is the polite plural imperative of کردن). The verb comes at the end as is normal in Persian.
segments.s762-g112: شب تیره is a noun phrase 'the dark night' (شب = night, تیره = dark/ominous) placed before the verb for emphasis. او را is the object 'him' with را marking definiteness/emphasis. ستایش کنید is again a plural imperative 'praise/worship (do praise)'. The line orders adverbial/time phrase + object + verb.
segments.s762-g41: همه بودنی‌ها is a fronted nominal phrase ('all existents / all that is') serving as the object/topic. ببینم is first-person singular present/subjunctive (classical/epic present) meaning 'I see/behold'; in classical Persian this form can express habitual or ongoing action. همی is a classical adverbial particle placed after the verb to mark continuous or habitual aspect ('continually/always'), so the clause reads literally 'I continually behold all beings.'
segments.s762-g42: وزان is a fused prepositional phrase meaning 'and from that / from it' that links this clause to the previous sighting. خامشی is the noun 'silence' as the thing chosen. برگزینم is first‑person singular present/subjunctive 'I choose/select' (classical present used for habitual action), and the trailing همی again marks the continuous/habitual sense ('I continually choose'). The literal order is 'from that silence I choose (always)'.
segments.s762-g43: بر ایرانیان is a prepositional phrase marking the target/occasion ('for/toward the Iranians') and appears before the predicative adjectives. زار و گریان are coordinate predicate adjectives 'wretched and weeping' linked by و. شدم is the past auxiliary 'I became' (first person past), so the structure is literally 'For the Iranians (I) became wretched and weeping.'
segments.s762-g44: ز ساسانیان is a prepositional phrase 'from / because of the Sasanians' placed at the clause start for emphasis; نیز is the additive adverb 'also/likewise'. بریان is a predicate adjective here meaning 'burnt/consumed' in a figurative, emotional sense, and شدم is again the past 'I became' — literally 'From the Sasanians I likewise became consumed.'
segments.s762-g45: دریغ is an exclamatory noun ('pity/alas') used here as an interjection. این modifies the following nouns ('this'), so این سر و تاج lists 'this head and crown'; then و این داد و تخت repeats the pattern for 'this justice and throne.' The phrase is a paratactic lament naming the principal symbols of rulership.
segments.s762-g46: Again دریغ begins the exclamation. این بزرگی 'this greatness' is followed by و این فر و بخت 'and this farr (royal glory) and fortune/fate.' The nouns are presented in apposition to lament what is lost; فر here carries its technical royal/auspicious sense.
segments.s762-g47: کزین پس is a temporal/causal phrase 'from this / after this', setting the time-frame. شکست آید is an impersonal construction with شکست (defeat) as the substantive subject and آید (third-person present/future) 'will come / will happen' as the verb: 'defeat will come.' از تازیان marks the source/agent 'from the Arabs.'
segments.s762-g48: ستاره is the subject 'the star' (a metonym for fortune). نگردد is the negative third‑person present/subjunctive/optative of گردیدن (here 'to turn/change'), giving a future/inevitable sense 'will not turn / will not change.' مگر introduces the exception 'except/unless,' and بر زیان is a prepositional phrase 'toward/on loss' indicating direction — so 'the star will not change except toward loss.'
segments.s762-g217: دو دیده is a noun phrase 'two eyes' (numeral + noun) serving as the topic/subject. ز is the classical preposition 'from/of' used here in a poetic/genitive sense linking to شاه جهان 'king of the world' (a genitive compound). برمدار functions as a predicative/adjectival phrase ('fixed/upon, under the sway of' in older diction) with an omitted copula (باشـد) typical of Persian poetry — it says essentially that the two eyes are to be held/kept toward the king.
segments.s762-g218: فدی کن is an imperative construction: فدی (sacrifice/ransom) used with the imperative کن 'do/make' — together 'sacrifice!' or 'offer as a ransom'. تن خویش is the direct object 'your body/person' where خوی + ش is the possessive form (خویش = 'one's own'); در کارزار is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the battlefield/in battle') modifying the action.
segments.s762-g219: که at the start introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause ('for/that'). زود آید is a temporal statement — زود 'soon' modifies the verb آید (present/future 'will come' or 'comes'). این روز آهرمنی is the noun phrase 'this demonic day' (این = 'this', روز = 'day', آهرمنی = 'demonic/malignant') serving as the subject of آید.
segments.s762-g220: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when'. گردون گردان is a nominal phrase 'the revolving/turning sky/heaven' (گردون 'sky, fate' modified by گردان 'revolving/turning'). کند is the verbal auxiliary 'makes/does' and دشمنی is the object/abstract noun 'enmity' — together 'when the turning heaven makes enmity' (i.e. when fate/sky turns hostile).
segments.s762-g221: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. نامه is the direct object 'letter' and به مهر is an adverbial/phrasing 'with a seal / sealed with affection' (به + مهر). اندر آورد is a compound past tense 'brought in / brought' (آورد as the verb) and گفت is the reporting verb 'he said' introducing the reported clause that follows.
segments.s762-g222: که here introduces reported content ('that'). پوینده is the subject 'the seeker/messenger'. با آفرین جفت is a predicative phrase in an optative construction: باد is the optative particle 'may' and جفت 'pair/mate' is the predicate ('be paired with'); با آفرین = 'with praise/acclamation' — so the whole clause expresses a wish: 'that the seeker may be paired with praise.'
segments.s762-g223: که again introduces a reported/integrated clause. این نامه is 'this letter' and نزد برادر is a prepositional phrase 'to/into the presence of the brother' (نزد = 'in the presence of/with'). برد here functions as the verb 'take/carry' in the clause (classical narrative often uses برد for 'carry/take' even if the lemma list gave other senses), so the clause means 'that he take this letter to his brother.'
segments.s762-g224: بگوید is a subjunctive/optative-form reporting verb 'let him say / he should say' (third-person subjunctive), governing the following content. جزین 'except/aside from this' limits the scope and هرچ اندرخورد is a noun phrase 'whatever is fitting' (هرچ = 'whatever', اندرخورد = 'fitting, appropriate'), so the meaning is 'and tell him, aside from this, whatever is fitting.'
segments.s762-g113: بکوشید is a second-person plural/polite exhortative (imperative/subjunctive) from کوشیدن meaning “strive/try,” placed at the start as the main verb. و links two coordinated imperatives, so بخشنده باشید is the second exhortation: بخشنده is an adjective “generous,” and باشید is the corresponding subjunctive/imperative form of بودن “be.” نیز at the end means “also/likewise,” adding the second injunction to the first.
segments.s762-g114: ز here is the poetic preposition “from/of” introducing the partitive object (i.e. “anything of eating”). خوردن is a verbal noun (masdar) “eating.” به فردا mманید is a negative exhortation: ممانید (negative of ماندن/ماندن in subjunctive/imperative) = “do not delay/do not remain”; به فردا = “to/toward tomorrow,” so the phrase means “do not put off (it) to tomorrow.” چیز (“thing/anything”) is an indefinite object emphasising that nothing of the eating should be postponed.
segments.s762-g115: که introduces an explanatory/causal clause (“for/that”), giving the reason or circumstance. من با سپاهی = “I, with (an) army/with my soldiers” (با + noun = accompaniment). به سختی is an adverbial phrase “with hardship/with difficulty” (به used adverbially here), and درم is the noun “dirham” — here idiomatically indicating a paltry sum or the speaker’s scant resources (i.e. left with but a dirham).
segments.s762-g116: به again marks the following state (“in/by/with”); رنج و غم و شوربختی is a triad of nouns listing the speaker’s condition: “suffering and sorrow and misfortune.” The final درم repeats the small-coin image for emphasis (i.e. suffering for the sake of / because of a dirham or having only a dirham while suffering). The clause continues and reinforces the hardship introduced in the previous segment.
segments.s762-g117: رهایی is a noun “release/freedom.” نیابم is the first-person singular (negative) form of یافتن/یابیدن ( here negated with initial ن-) meaning “I do not find” or “I cannot obtain.” سرانجام is an adverb “ultimately/in the end,” and از + ین (ازین = از + این) together mean “from this.” Word order places the negated verb with its object/adjuncts following it in typical classical Persian style.
segments.s762-g118: خوشا باد is a fixed optative phrase: خوشا = “how wonderful” and باد is the optative particle “may it be.” نوشین is a poetic adjective meaning “sweet/delightful,” placed before the noun it qualifies, ایران‌زمین = “land of Iran.” Together the phrase expresses a blessing/wish: “May the sweet land of Iran be (well)!”
segments.s762-g119: چو is the temporal conjunction “when.” گیتی is the subject “the world/the world-order,” شود is the present/future-form of شدن “become,” and تنگ is the predicative adjective “narrow/tight.” بر شهریار = “for/upon the king” indicates who is affected; the clause means “when the world becomes constrained for the king” (i.e. when times grow hard for a ruler).
segments.s762-g120: تو is the addressed “you.” گنج و تن و جان lists three things—“treasure, body, and life”—as direct objects. گرامی is an adjective “dear/precious.” مدار here functions as an imperative/prohibitive particle in classical/poetic usage meaning “do not make/consider (so)” (i.e. “do not hold dear”); although the lemma senses given are nominal, in context مدار is used verbally/imperatively and negated by the idiomatic sense of the clause (“do not deem … precious”).
segments.s762-g161: This clause has inverted word order typical of classical Persian: the predicate adjective 'بداندیش' (ill‑minded) comes first, followed by the verb 'گردد' (become), and finally the actor 'پدر' with the prepositional phrase 'بر پسر' indicating target/against whom. 'بر' here means 'against/toward' and links the father's attitude to the son.
segments.s762-g162: 'پسر بر پدر' mirrors the previous order (son against the father). 'هم‌چنین' (likewise) modifies the following predicate 'چاره‌گر'. In this poetic context 'چاره‌گر' functions as 'one who takes measures / contrives' (i.e., plots or retaliates), so the son is said to respond in kind. Word order places the actor before the target, then the adverb, then the predicate noun.
segments.s762-g163: Here the verb 'شود' (becomes) opens the clause and is followed by the predicate noun phrase 'بنده بی‌هنر' (an unskilled servant). The title 'شهریار' (sovereign/king) appears at the end as the logical subject; classical Persian allows this postverbal placement of the subject for emphasis or meter.
segments.s762-g164: The coordinate noun phrase 'نژاد و بزرگی' ('lineage and greatness') is the semantic theme; the negative verb 'نیاید' (does not come / will not avail) is used idiomatically with 'به کار' ('to work/for use') to mean 'is of no use' or 'will not help.' The whole clause states that ancestry and grandeur will not help in that case.
segments.s762-g165: The locative phrase 'به گیتی' ('in the world') opens the line. The construction 'کسی را نماند وفا' is a classical syntactic pattern where 'را' marks 'کسی' as the indirect object and 'نماند' (does not remain) governs 'وفا' (fidelity): literally, 'fidelity does not remain for anyone.' The negative sense ('no one') is carried by context and the verb.
segments.s762-g166: This clause coordinates two items 'روان و زبان‌ها' (souls and tongues/speeches) as subjects; 'شود' (becomes) is the copular/auxiliary verb and 'پر جفا' means 'full of cruelty/injustice' where 'پر' is the adjective 'full (of)'. Together: 'souls and tongues become full of cruelty.'
segments.s762-g167: This is a simple enumerative prepositional series: 'از' ('from') repeated before each group for emphasis—'ایران' (Iran), 'ترک' (Turks), 'تازیان' (Arabs). The repeated preposition stresses origin/source.
segments.s762-g168: 'نژادی' (a race/by descent) here functions as an indefinite noun meaning 'a people/a race'; 'پدید آید' is the compound verb 'appear/arise' (پدید + آید). 'اندر میان' is a locative phrase 'amid/among' and completes the thought: "a race will appear among them." Word order places the noun first and the verbal phrase after for emphasis.
segments.s762-g89: This is a paired prepositional phrase using the poetic preposition ز (z) twice to mean “from” or “whether from … or …”: ز زابلستان‌گر ز ایران سپاه = “from Zabulistan‑gar, (or) from Iran, (the) host…”. زابلستان‌گر is an agentive/national designation (“one of Zabulistan” / “a Zabulistani”) placed after the first ز; ایران follows the second ز; سپاه (“host/army”) is the head noun. The word order (z + place/agent, z + place, head noun) is a characteristic poetic alternation where both sources are given before the noun they qualify.
segments.s762-g90: هرآنکس که introduces a free relative clause “whoever/anyone who …”; که is the subordinating particle linking the clause to the verb. آیند is third‑person plural present/subjunctive of آمدن used here as the verb of the relative clause (“come/arrive”); زنهارخواه is a compound noun meaning “one who begs for mercy / a suppliant.” The whole phrase functions as the continuation of the fronted description: “whoever comes as a suppliant.”
segments.s762-g91: This is a string of imperatives joined by و (and): بدار, بپوش, بیارای. Each verb is an instruction: بدار (keep/hold/maintain), بپوش (put on/wear), بیارای (adorn/make beautiful). مهر here is the object/goal of the last verb and means “love/kindness/favor” — i.e. “adorn with favor” or “show kindness.” In poetic usage imperatives can be succinct and stacked like this to give a sequence of commands.
segments.s762-g92: نگه کن is an imperative compound: نگه (gaze/watch/keeping) + کن (do!) = “keep watch” or “look/observe.” بدین is the prepositional/demonstrative sequence (ب- + دین) meaning “at/onto these” (poetic form of ‘to these’). گردگردان سپهر is a nominative phrase “the whirling/turning sphere(s) of heaven” (گردگردان = turning/whirling, سپهر = sky/heaven). The whole phrase therefore commands: “keep watch upon these whirling heavens.”
segments.s762-g93: This is an elliptical clause where the copula is omitted: ازو شادمانی = “from him (is) joy,” and زو در نهیب = “and from him (is) a rebuke.” ازو/زو are prepositional pronouns “from him/it,” شادمانی is the noun “joy,” در here functions as a prepositional/locative marker leading into نهیب (“rebuke” or a loud reproving cry). The two balanced parts contrast favor and admonition coming from the same source.
segments.s762-g94: زمانی فرازست is a temporal comment: زمانی (“for a while” / “at one time”) + فرازست (copula + adjective/verb meaning “is high/exalted” or “is uplifted”). The second half, و روزی نشیب, parallels it: روزی (“a day/at another time”) + نشیب (“descent/low point”). The construction describes alternating fortune: sometimes high, sometimes low.
segments.s762-g95: سخن هرچ گفتم is a nominalized clause “the words whatever I said” (سخن = speech/words, هرچ = whatever, گفتم = ‘I said’ – here used elliptically as a relative content clause). به مادر بگوی is an imperative: به (to) مادر (mother) + بگوی (tell/say) — “tell it to my mother.” The order places the content first and then the imperative to inform the mother.
segments.s762-g96: نبیند is negative + verb (ن‑ + بیند) meaning “she/he will not see” or “does not see” (third person). همانا is an emphatic particle “verily/indeed.” The sequence م را is the object pronoun + object marker (here representing مرا “me”); نیز is “also/too”; روی is “face.” Together: “Indeed she will not see my face either.” Note the classical/poetic splits and the omitted copula in the clause.
segments.s762-g201: مرا + را here (written separately as م / را) functions as the accusative/dative clitic meaning “to me / for me” and marks the experiencer of the following wish. کاشکی is an optative particle meaning “if only / would that” that introduces a desire; it governs the whole clause. این خرد is a noun phrase “this wisdom” (خرد = wisdom) and نیستی is the negative copula in an optative/irrealis sense (“were not / did not exist”), so the clause literally says “Would that this wisdom were not to me.”
segments.s762-g202: گر is the conditional particle “if.” اندیشه نیک و بد is a coordinated noun phrase “thought/notion of good and bad” (اندیشه = thought; نیک = good; بد = bad). The clause ends with نیستی, again the negative copula used irrealis/optative here to express the wished absence of such discriminating thought: “if there were no thought of good and evil.”
segments.s762-g203: بزرگان is the plural noun “the great ones / nobles.” که is the relative conjunction “who.” در + قادسی is a locative phrase “in Qadsi” (قادسی here marks a place or epithet), and بامنند (با + من + ـند) is the verb phrase meaning “are with me” (older orthography links the pieces; the final ـند is the 3rd-person plural copula). The whole segment is a relative clause: “the great ones who are with me in Qadsi.”
segments.s762-g204: درشتند is a past/state descriptive predicate “are/appear stout, severe, or fierce” (here describing their bearing). و is the coordinating conjunction “and.” بر + تازیان + (دشمن + ند) gives the second predicate: بر تازیان دشمنند = “they are hostile/tend to act against the Arabs” (بر indicates direction/against; دشمن is predicate noun; ـند is the 3rd-person plural copula).
segments.s762-g205: گمانند is a 3rd-person plural present tense ‘they suppose / they imagine.’ کاین is the fused conjunction+demonstrative (‘that this’), introducing the content clause. بیش بیرون شود is a small subordinate clause: بیش = “any more / farther,” بیرون = “out / forth,” and شود is the present subjunctive/impersonal verb “become / go / happen.” Together: “they imagine that this will go out any farther.”
segments.s762-g206: ز is the poetic preposition “from / due to.” دشمن زمین رود is a terse sequence: دشمن = ‘the enemy,’ زمین = ‘the land,’ and رود (here a verb-form spelled like the noun) functions as ‘will go / will leave’ (older Persian often compresses verb forms), so the clause says the enemy will leave the land. جیحون شود follows as a separate result clause: جیحون (the Amu Darya) + شود (“will become / will be affected”), giving the sense of exaggeration: if they go farther, the enemy will leave the land and the Jayhun will be (something drastic).
segments.s762-g207: ز here is the poetic preposition “of / concerning.” راز سپهری is a genitive phrase “the secret of the heavens / the celestial secret” (سپهری = celestial). کس آگاه نیست is an existential negative construction: کس = “anyone / no one,” آگاه = “aware,” and نیست = “is not,” so the segment states that no one is aware of this heavenly secret.
segments.s762-g208: ندانند is the negative present of دانستن in 3rd-person plural (“they do not know”). کاین introduces the content clause “that this ….” رنج کوتاه نیست is a simple predication: رنج = “suffering / hardship,” کوتاه = “short,” and نیست = “is not,” so the clause says “they do not know that this hardship is not short,” i.e. it will not be brief.
segments.s762-g137: This is a negated list introduced by نه ... نه ... (neither ... nor ...). The verb بینی (see) follows the listed nouns, so the sequence is noun phrases (تخت, دیهیم, شهر) followed by the verb 'you see' — a common classical/poetic word order where the verb comes after its objects. دیهیم here is a noun 'villager/rural person' rather than an adjective.
segments.s762-g138: ز اختر ('from/by the star') gives a causal or locative prepositional phrase; همه تازیان is the subject ('all the Arabs') and راست بهر is a short adverbial phrase meaning 'straight/toward (the) star' or 'directly toward' — راست modifies their orientation. Word order is compact/poetic: prepositional phrase + subject + directional adverb.
segments.s762-g139: چو introduces a temporal clause ('when'); روز اندر آید literally places the noun 'day' before the verb 'comes' with the particle اندر ('in/within') between, a typical classical order. به‌روز دراز is a compressed descriptive phrase ('the day becomes long' / 'the day stretches long'), where به‌روز is an adverbial/qualifying element ('of the day' / 'upon the day').
segments.s762-g140: شود is a third-person form of 'to become' (here functioning as a light verb meaning 'it happens / becomes that'); ناسزا is the noun 'insult/abuse' and functions as the event affecting the following noun phrase شاه گردن‌فراز ('the proud king'). In this construction the patient/target (شاه گردن‌فراز) appears after the verbal phrase, giving a passive/experiential sense: 'the proud king is met with abuse.'
segments.s762-g141: بپوشد is a third-person present/subjunctive form of 'to put on/wear' used narratively; it appears before its logical subject (گروهی) — classical Persian often places the verb before the subject. از ایشان ('of/from them') indicates that the group is drawn from 'them' (i.e. some among them), and گروهی سیاه means 'a group (wearing) black' or 'some in black.'
segments.s762-g142: ز دیبا ('from/of brocade') gives the material of the hats; نهند is the verb 'they place/set' (third-person plural), and از بر سر کلاه is a prepositional phrase meaning 'a hat upon the head' — note the older poetic combination از بر meaning 'on/upon.' The sequence literally: 'they place (hats) of brocade upon the head.'
segments.s762-g143: Another negated coordinate string using نه ... و نه ... و نه ... ('neither ... nor ... nor ...'). The nouns تخت, تاج, زرینه‌کفش are listed as absent. The repeated و simply links the items; the negation نه applies distributively to the whole list.
segments.s762-g144: Continues the negated inventory: نه گوهر نه افسر lists more missing items ('no jewel, no helmet'), and نه بر سر درفش adds that even a banner (درفش) is not on the head — بر سر is the prepositional phrase 'upon the head.' The construction maintains the same negative scope across the final three nouns.
segments.s762-g185: چو here is a temporal conjunction meaning 'when' (not simile or conditional). The phrase is built as a subordinate temporal clause with the verb بگذرد (3rd person singular present/subjunctive) placed at the end, a typical poetic order: 'when much of this tale passes.' ازین is the fused prepositional phrase ('از' + orthographic variant 'ین' = 'این') meaning 'of/from this.'
segments.s762-g186: کسی is used in the negative sense 'no one' because the verb is prefixed by ن- (ننگرد), producing a negated 3rd-person form 'does not look/turn.' سوی آزادگی is a prepositional phrase 'toward freedom,' and the verb ننگرد (negated present/optative) governs the clause, yielding 'no one turns/looks toward freedom.'
segments.s762-g187: بریزند is a 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive form 'they pour/shed' used here in a generalizing, narrative sense. از پی is a fixed prepositional expression 'in pursuit of/after,' so از پی خواسته means 'in pursuit of desire' (خواسته as a noun 'wish/desire'). The object خون follows the verb, a common Persian order in poetry.
segments.s762-g188: شود is the impersonal/auxiliary 3rd-person singular 'becomes / happens.' The order is inverted for poetic effect: روزگار (the times/day) is the logical subject, modified by مهان (guests/visitors), and کاسته is a predicative adjective 'diminished.' Literally: 'the times become diminished of guests.'
segments.s762-g189: This is a coordinate clause: دل من 'my heart' is the subject, پر از خون شد uses پر از + noun with شد (past/become) to mean 'became full of blood.' The conjunction و links the result clause 'روی زرد' where the verb شد is implicitly applied to 'روی' as well, so 'my face grew pale.'
segments.s762-g190: دهن خشک is an adjectival phrase 'mouth (is) dry.' The conjunction و connects it to لب‌ها شده لاژورد, where شده (become/has become) applies to 'لب‌ها' giving 'the lips have become lapis-lazuli' (a poetic way to say bluish/turned blue). Note the verb aparece only once (شده) for the final predicate.
segments.s762-g191: که تا combines a subordinating sense ('that/by the time') with تا indicating a temporal limit 'until / by the time.' من شدم پهلوان is simple past 'I became a champion,' and از میان ('from among') gives the sense 'among them' or 'from among (people).' The clause sets the temporal point for the result clause that follows.
segments.s762-g192: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' and introduces the result: تیره شد بخت ساسانیان 'the fortune of the Sasanids became dark/ominous.' Note the inverted, emphatic order (adverb + predicate + possessor), with بخت ساسانیان a genitival phrase 'the fate/fortune of the Sasanids.'
segments.s762-g1: This chunk lists the direct objects/people sent. Persian uses the object marker را after the (definite) direct object: عمر سعد وقاس را = 'ʿUmar Saʿd and Waqas (object)'. با سپاه is a prepositional phrase 'with an army' that follows the object and marks accompaniment.
segments.s762-g2: فرستاد is a simple past verb 'he sent/dispatched'. تا introduces a purpose clause here ('so that / in order that') and governs the subjunctive/intentive جوید (jooyad) = 'seek/engage in'. ز here is the poetic preposition 'against/from' linking جنگ ('battle') to شاه ('the king').
segments.s762-g3: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. آگاه شد is a past‑tense periphrasis 'became aware' (آگاه = aware; شد = became). زان ('of that / from that') modifies سخن ('word/report'), and یزدگرد is the subject being described.
segments.s762-g4: This clause gives the action: ز هر سو = 'from every side' (prepositional phrase of source/direction), سپاه = 'the army/forces', اندر آورد = past 'brought in/assembled' (اندر 'in/among' + آورد 'brought'), and گرد functions as an adverbial/poetic particle 'around/about', completing the image of forces gathering from all directions.
segments.s762-g5: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb often used to mean 'he commanded/said' in classical Persian. تا introduces the subordinate instruction ('that ...'), پور هرمزد is the person ordered (Pūr Hormozd = 'son of Hormozd' used as a name), and راه is the direct object of پیماییدن ('to traverse/take the road').
segments.s762-g6: بپیماید is an optative/subjunctive or jussive form 'should traverse / (may) traverse' following تا in an instructive sense. و = 'and' links the two verbs; برکشد is a poetic verb meaning roughly 'set out / lift/advance' (here 'advance' or 'march forward with'), and با سپاه 'with the army' marks accompaniment.
segments.s762-g7: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause about the person named (رستم). بدش here is an archaic/poetic form: بُد (was) fused with the enclitic ش ('his'), so the phrase links to نام ('name/reputation'); thus بدش نام = 'his name/reputation was ...'. و بیدار بود is a coordinated predicate 'and he was awake/alert' with بود as the past copula.
segments.s762-g8: This is a predicate list formed by adjectives/nouns joined by و and closed by the copula بود: خردمند = 'wise', (و) گرد = an archaic epithet (here a laudatory title; not covered by the supplied senses), و جهاندار = 'world-ruler/sovereign', and بود = 'was'. The sequence gives Rostam's qualities in apposition.
segments.s762-g121: کزین is the contracted poetic preposition 'از این' meaning 'from this/of this'. تخمه is the head noun 'lineage/offspring' followed by two postpositive adjectives نامدار (renowned) and ارجمند (esteemed); Persian allows adjectives to follow the noun for emphasis in poetry, so the phrase reads 'this renowned, esteemed stock.'
segments.s762-g122: نماندست is a poetic contracted negative predicate (نماند + است) literally 'remained not' = 'there remains none'. The exception is introduced by جز and follows the verb in the typical Persian inverted/poetic order: 'nothing remains except شهریار بلند' where بلند is an honorific adjective 'lofty/exalted' modifying شهریار.
segments.s762-g123: ز کوشش is a prepositional phrase ('in/with effort' or 'regarding effort') and مکن is the negative imperative 'do not'. هیچ سستی به کار is the object phrase 'any/slight slackness in action' (هیچ = 'any/no'), so the whole clause commands 'do not show any laxity in your effort/work.'
segments.s762-g124: This clause is inverted: 'به گیتی' ('in the world') sets the location, جزو نیستمان یادگار states 'there is no part of us that is a memorial.' نیستمان is the poetic fused copula/negation 'is not (for us/left)', so the sense is 'there remains no memorial of us in the world.'
segments.s762-g125: ز ساسانیان marks provenance 'from the Sasanians.' یادگار اوست is a nominal clause with اوست (او + است) as copula: 'he/it is a memorial.' بس here is a limiting/emphatic particle 'enough' or 'sufficient', so the line says 'He is a sufficient memorial of the Sasanians.'
segments.s762-g126: کزین پس is a temporal phrase 'after this' (کز + این + پس). نبینند is a third-person plural negative form used here to mean 'they will not see' or more idiomatically 'no one will be seen'; the following nominal phrase 'زین تخمه کس' is the affected group 'anyone of this stock.' Note: the listed senses for the word token زین in the lemma list don't match the usage here, so its semantic mapping is nonstandard in that list.
segments.s762-g127: دریغ is an exclamatory lament 'alas' or 'pity.' It introduces a list of valued things: این سر و تاج (this head and crown) و این مهر و داد (and this kindness/favor and justice). The nouns are simple coordinated objects of the lament; the repetition of این emphasizes 'these very things.'
segments.s762-g128: که here introduces a result/content clause 'that ...'. خواهدشد is the future/impersonal compound 'will become/happen' (contracted in poetry). این تخت شاهی به باد is the predicate: 'this royal throne (تخت شاهی) to the wind' — the idiom به باد آمدن/شدن meaning 'to be ruined/lost.' So the clause says 'that this kingship/throne will be blown away (lost).'
segments.s762-g193: چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus/so' placed before the predicate for emphasis. بی‌وفا is an adjectival predicate 'unfaithful/disloyal'. گشت is the past/inchoative verb 'became/turned' (here intransitive), and گردان‌سپهر is the poetic compound subject 'the revolving heavens' (literally 'the turning sphere') that the predicate refers to.
segments.s762-g194: دژم گشت is a second short clause repeating the intransitive verb گشت with the adjective دژم 'sullen/grim' (so: 'became sullen'). وز here is the archaic preposition/particle (written as و + ز) meaning 'from/and from' (taking the sense 'from' in context) — not one of the gust/weight senses listed, so it is annotated below as -1; ما is the dative/oblique 'us' (for/from us), ببرید is the past form meaning 'took away' (برد as 'take/carry'), and مهر is the object 'affection/love'. The clause means 'it became sullen and took love from us.'
segments.s762-g195: مرا is the dative/accusative 'me/for me' (here functioning as 'for me'); the words were split as م + را in the word list but combine to the single prosodic object 'مرا'. را is the object marker attached to that pronoun. تیز modifies پیکان as 'sharp' (a quality of an arrow), and آهن‌گذار is a compound meaning 'iron‑setter' / 'iron‑tipped' or 'one who fastens iron' (used adjectivally here to indicate an iron‑pointed arrow).
segments.s762-g196: همی is the classical preverbal particle indicating continued/habitual action ('still/indeed' here shading into 'is (in) no use'). بر is the preposition 'against' governing برهنه 'the unarmored/naked' as its object. نیاید is the negative present/optative form of آمدن used here with the sense 'does not come / is not fitting' (so 'is of no use'). به کار is the idiom به کار آمدن 'to be of use' (به = to/for; کار = use/function). Altogether: 'is of no use against the unarmored.'
segments.s762-g197: همان is the demonstrative 'that very / the same'. تیغ is the noun 'sword'. کز is the relative particle 'that/which' introducing the relative clause (here 'which did not spare ...'); گردن is 'neck' and پیل و شیر are the two coordinated nouns 'elephant and lion'. The whole segment sets up 'the sword which ...'.
segments.s762-g198: نگشتی is the negative past/inchoative sense of گشت here meaning 'did not turn / did not spare / did not fail' (i.e., it did not refrain from striking). به آورد is the verbal sequence with به introducing purpose/result and آورد (past of آوردن) 'brought/produced'. زان is a contraction 'from that' (from that wound), زخم is 'wound', and سیر is 'satiation/satisfaction' (so 'bring satisfaction from that wound'). The clause says that even that sword failed to bring any satisfaction from that wound.
segments.s762-g199: نبرد is the noun 'battle' placed at the clause head; in poetry a bare noun like this can mean 'battle after battle' or 'the fighting'. همی is the continuous particle 'kept/continually', پوست is the noun 'skin' (here what is stripped), بر is the preposition 'against/over' and تازیان names the subject 'the Arabs' (object of the flaying). The clause conveys 'the fighting kept flaying the Arabs' (i.e., they were repeatedly skinned/defeated).
segments.s762-g200: ز is the poetic form of 'از' meaning 'from/because of' and introduces the cause 'دانش' (knowledge). زیان is 'loss/detriment'. آمدم is the first‑person past 'I came' (here idiomatically 'I suffered / it befell me'), and بر زیان 'upon loss' repeats زیان for the sense 'loss upon loss'. The sentence means 'because of knowledge I suffered loss after loss.'
segments.s762-g81: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause meaning "when" (not simile). نامه بخوانی is a present/subjunctive 2nd-person clause "you read" (poetic use of بخوانی for a time-condition). خرد را is the direct object with را marking definiteness/emphasis; مران here functions as a proper name/vocative (addressing someone named Maran) who is the recipient or addressee of "wisdom."
segments.s762-g82: بپرداز is an imperative (or jussive) verb meaning "attend to / deal with." و simply coordinates two verbs. برساز is another imperative "set up / put in order / prepare." با introduces the company/means "with," and مهتران (an archaic/plural form of مهتر) means "the great/elders/chiefs" — here "arrange it together with the chiefs."
segments.s762-g83: همه گرد کن is an imperative instruction: همه ("everyone") + گرد (adverb/particle here meaning "around/together") + کن (imperative "make/do") = "bring everyone together." خواسته هرچ هست is a relative/descriptive clause "whatever is desired/wished" (خواسته = "wish/desire," هرچ = "whatever," هست = copula "is").
segments.s762-g84: پرستنده (here collective) names the people doing the action: "the worshipper(s)/devotees/attendants." و simply links phrases. جامه برنشست is an older/poetic phrasing: جامه = "garment/robe," and برنشست (poetic past-type verb) conveys that they rose/assumed or sat up with their garments — practically rendered as "they rose and donned their garments."
segments.s762-g85: همی is a frequentative/adverbial particle giving continuity ("kept / still"). تاز here is the root of تازیدن "to rush/charge" used verbally in poetry (the supplied senses list did not include the verbal sense). تا + آذرآبادگان expresses extent/limit: "as far as / up to Ādarābādgān." Together this says "continue charging/pressing on up to Ādarābādgān."
segments.s762-g86: به here marks direction/goal "to/into." جای is "place/seat/rank" (here: "the place/rank of"). بزرگان = "the great ones / nobles" and آزادگان = "the free / noble-minded men." The phrase means "to the place/seats of the great and the free."
segments.s762-g87: همیدون is a proper name (vocative/imperative address to Hamidun). گله هرچ داری ز اسپ is a noun phrase with quantifier and possession: گله = "a herd/pack," هرچ داری = "whatever you have" (هرچ + داری), ز اسپ = "of horses / from horses" (ز = "of/from," اسپ = "horse"). So the whole segment is addressed to Hamidun: "(take) whatever herd of horses you have."
segments.s762-g88: ببر is the imperative "take!" (here commanding to lead/take the herd). سوی marks direction "toward(s)." گنجور is "treasury / treasure-house" (here a place), and آذرگشسپ is the proper name attached to that place (the treasure-house of Azargashasp). The segment thus commands: "take (them) toward the treasure-house of Azargashasp."
segments.s762-g145: به رنج is an adverbial prepositional phrase ('by/with hardship') placed before the clause. یکی دیگری are two indefinite pronouns in sequence meaning 'one (person) [meets] another'; classical Persian often places subject and object adjacent without a copula. برخورد is the lexical verb 'encounter/meet' in Ferdowsi's poetic diction (bare third‑person form) and gives the clause a simple past/habitual sense: 'one meets another.'
segments.s762-g146: This clause has two parallel prepositional objects (به داد و به بخشش) 'to/for justice and to/for generosity' followed by همی‌ننگرد. همی‑ is a classical habitual/present prefix; ننگرد is the negative form of 'to look/regard' in Ferdowsi's style, so the whole verb means 'does not look/pay heed' (habitual). The verb is third‑person and the negative prefix ن‑ attaches directly to the verb stem.
segments.s762-g147: شب آید is a temporal clause ('when/night comes') with the verb آید (third‑person). یکی is an indefinite subject 'someone'; چشمه رخشان is a noun phrase 'a radiant/bright spring' with the adjective رخشان post‑nominal, and کند is the verb 'makes/causes' (classical present/habitual form) — so 'someone causes a radiant spring.'
segments.s762-g148: نهفته کسی را places نهفته ('hidden') before کسی را: the latter with را marks a definite object ('someone's hidden [thing]' or 'the hidden thing of someone'). خروشان is an adjectival/predicative complement 'roaring, turbulent' and کند is the same causative/active verb 'makes' — together 'he makes someone's hidden thing roar.'
segments.s762-g149: ستاننده is a present‑participle/agent noun 'the taker/collector'. روزشان is a possessive construction 'their day(s)' (روز + ـشان). دیگرست is the contracted predicate 'is other/different' (دیگر + است), so the clause reads 'the taker of their days is someone else.'
segments.s762-g150: This is a copular/descriptive clause using two prepositional phrases: 'کمر بر میان' ('the waist is at the middle') and 'کله بر سرست' ('the head is on the top'). بر + noun gives location 'on/at'; the final سرست is an archaic fused form (سر + است) functioning as the predicate 'is on the head/top.' The two halves are joined by و.
segments.s762-g151: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' introducing what is abandoned ('ز پیمان' = 'from the covenant'). بگردند is a plural verb form meaning 'they turn/turn away' (classical present/subjunctive/plain form used for habitual/general actions). The following وز راستی is a coordinate/prepositional pairing meaning 'and/from uprightness' (وز here is an archaic connective/variant related to از; راستی is 'uprightness/truth').
segments.s762-g152: گرامی شود is a passive‑like construction 'becomes honoured/esteemed' (گرامی = 'honoured/valued', شود = 'becomes'). کژی و کاستی are the subjects/themes 'crookedness and deficiency' (joined by و) that, as a result, 'become esteemed.' The verb is third‑person singular and impersonal in shape.
